text: Philippe Crahay,, Climate and Resilience Service, part of PPGR. Everything anticipatory is under Resilience Unit. Recently restructured.. There is different targeting for emergency and resilience targeting.. In emergency, you start at point X when shock hits and identify those most in need / those you want to reach.. Then you try to spread your reach and go broad,, typically. But for resilience targeting, the rational is a bit different.. - Theory: Target areas at interface of chronic exposure to FSC, high exposure to shocks.. We look at trend of FSC over no. of years.. E..g.. focusing on areas that are frequently hit by drought, etc.. and high recurrent food insecurity.. O -> Typically 3-5 Ys of intergrated efforts in the same communities, in the same HHS.. So the targeting approach is typically community-based or self-selected.  we don’t have a unified targeting approach. Or resilience! Most of our resilience programming sprouts out of areas where we have emergency programs already.. We have limited no. of successful resilience programmes. Barriers to reach the above targeting approach. Externally,, the main barrier is annual funding and increasing earmarking. Flagship Programme SAHEL Resilience Initiative.. New policy: resilience is supposed to lead to reduction of food assistance needs.  We intervene in areas where there is repeated food insecurity.. - Practice: we don’t have the normative framework established.. We have looser connection with VAM, who is running the show on targeting.. The policy is a big opportunity for us, also this evaluation. To connect the resilience work more to saving lives.. - Good practice: CBPP – some governments / NGOs/ UN use it.. WFP is a leader on this,, comes from asset creation type initiatives. Typically not WFP directly conducting this.. But we brief partners, other orgs on it.. It’s not typically vulnerability driven,, but we aim for more holistic approach. Different philosophy.. O Niger: combine integrated context analysis with community-based approach, with authorities, etc... - We have one tool we use for targeting resilience: Integrated Context Analysis (ICA).. Stays at geographic level. Can go up to different admin levels depending on what you have in place.. No,, not really. Mostly community based and self-selection.. Resilience is not so affected by the ETH cases. 80% of WFP is emergency. 20% is “changing lives”. Of that 20%,, resilience is 5%.. So we are peanuts in these contexts. We need partners, long-term funding and investment,, etc.  People shouldn’t mix “changing lives” with “resilience”.. The CSPs are confusing in this regard,,, a lot of confusion within WFP about this.. Our scope: any intervention that includes food or cash.. Overall,,, how relevant / useful is the whole bunch of T/P policies for you?? We are outsiders. The ED circular doesn’t concern / mention us a lot.. Now that we have a new policy, it could be an opportunity to fix / improve our targeting and/or to make existing tools and guidance more relevant to our work.  This is also the expectation to this evaluation.. Do you use RAM data, APP data??? [Didn’t get response.]. Yes,,, we know what RAM has, and we adapt it for our purposes.. But this also differs from country to country.. Involvement of partners in targeting: Yes,, unless in conflict settings or where capacity is not there.. We hear more about targeting compared to prioritization. Both is mostly about how much to do / for how many people.. Is there also a discussion about how much resilience to do vs.. emergency?? Does resilience get cut to enable wider reach with emergency programming?? Yes,,, when budgets are tight, saving lives is prioritized.. Except when you have something earmarked for resilience. In the resilience agenda, we combine different pots of money.. We don’t have many donors like GER who are supporting us for multiple years. -> No rules to guide this, it’s up to Country Office and programmatic rational.. Note: there is really only GER (BMZ) funding multiple years (plus some climate finance).. Resilience often gets cut first, to allow for saving lives (within the guardrails of the overall country strategy).. - The evaluations OEV are leading have knock-on effect on programs., So we try to collaborate a lot with OEV before it goes institutional.. We’d like to see more analysis on resilience, specifically, on potential gaps,, recommendations how to close them.. - We have struggled to institutionalize a coherent targeting approach as prescribed by guidance.. Would be great to hear what we can do close the gap.. - How to better connect resilience and emergency? - Asset creation is essential to climate adaptation and resilience. He can connect us to colleagues working on that..
Document Title: Everything anticipatory is under resilience unit. Recently restructured.
